threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver doesn’t wait for you to sit down before pulling away.
Last night on the 333, a guy serenaded the whole bus with Whitney Houston while everyone else just stared. Only in Streatham, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a kebab shop open at 4 AM, with the owner chatting about politics while the late-night crowd lingered on the sidewalk.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bar that serves cocktails exclusively made from pickled vegetables. I swear, it's a whole vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they roll their eyes at the surprise of a BART delay—like, welcome to our Friday chaos. It’s just part of the routine.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the N-Judah powering through the Tenderloin, dodging a group of breakdancers taking over the station. Only in SF do you get a front-row seat to the show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all sip wine at 2 AM. Most Gràcians are home by midnight—just trying to keep up with work and the kids.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their hesitation at crossing the street. They still expect cars to stop at red lights—good luck with that!

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You start admiring the street art but still flinch at the buskers blasting pop hits near the station. That's a rookie move.
Saw a guy trying to barter with the driver for a free ride last night. Offered him a bag of chips. Driver just shook his head and laughed.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a saxophonist jamming with a kid on a skateboard in front of the Turkish grocery. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find spontaneous raves in abandoned buildings that no one acknowledges—like they’ve always been part of the landscape.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to stay clear of the Civic Center BART when the sun goes down; the weird sounds and smoke are never a good sign.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 tonight at Civic Center, but a dog decided it was his turn to ride first. Reminded me that only in SF does your commute include canine drama.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia’s vibrant squares are just for photos, but locals actually rely on them for meeting friends and enjoying a quiet café in the sun.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They'll be the ones staring at the metro map like it's a treasure map, trying to decode where the weekend market is.
